title: Disney Leads with R-Rated Fox Franchises as 'Alien' and 'Deadpool' Sequels Rule the Box Office
text: Disney continues its box office dominance with two R-rated franchises acquired from 20th Century Fox. "Alien: Romulus" opened at number one with $41.5 million, while "Deadpool & Wolverine" secured second place with $29 million in its fourth weekend. Both films represent Disney's strategic acquisition of Fox properties through its 2019 takeover. The studio noted that "Fox content has provided $825 million of Disney's $1.467 billion industry-leading gross so far this year." "Alien: Romulus" earned an additional $66.7 million internationally, including a $25.7 million debut in China. Sony's "It Ends with Us" claimed third place with $24 million, representing a 52 percent decline from its opening weekend but approaching an unexpected $100 million total. The overall weekend grossed approximately $136 million, marking a 36 percent increase from the same period last year. However, year-to-date performance remains down 15 percent. The top ten included several other performers: Universal's "Twisters" and "Despicable Me 4," Disney's "Inside Out 2," and notably, a "Coraline" reissue through Fathom that earned $11.3 million, described as "the company's highest take ever for a re-release."
